channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After raucous first week in office, Donald Trump to keep his foot on the gas
item.description: Following a busy first week of his second term, President Donald Trump has a number of key policies to advance as week two begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: List: All of Trump’s executive orders on the economy
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a flurry of policy actions since returning to the White House on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump should rethink revoking former officials' security details, Tom Cotton says
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — President Donald Trump should rethink his decision to remove security details from three former senior national security officials, the chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee said Sunday. That protection is not just for them, but also the public, said Sen.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems take ‘government for granted’ on immigration: Chicago alderman
item.description: "Many Democratic leaders refuse to show any kind of working relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump says he would like to see Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “clean out” the area to create a virtual clean slate.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exclusive: White House in talks to have Oracle and U.S. investors take over TikTok
item.description: The aim is to place oversight control in the hands of American software company Oracle and other investors. Under federal law, TikTok must split apart from China, or face a nationwide ban.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Florida Republican urges Trump to spare some migrants under deportation plan
item.description: Salazar, a daughter of Cuban exiles, represents Florida's 27th Congressional District which is home to a number of Venezuelans and Cubans.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: The CIA believes COVID most likely originated from a lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's DOJ asks Supreme Court to freeze cases on student debt, environment
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday asked the Supreme Court to freeze four pending cases implicating the environment or student debt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires 17 government watchdogs at various federal agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump fired more than a dozen government watchdogs late Friday in a move that appears to have violated federal law requiring him to notify Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian winemaker and US veterans team up to show the best of Ukraine, a glass at a time
item.description: MONTCLAIR, Va. (AP) — In a wine shop an hour outside of Washington, owner Arthur Lampros sampled a wine from a part of the world that was totally new to him, racking his brain to pin down the tastes on his tongue.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth says Pentagon will 'put America first' as sec. of defense
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's tiebreaking vote confirmed Pete Hegseth as secretary of defense under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats and voting groups say a bid to toss out North Carolina ballots is an attack on democracy
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Copland Rudolph cast a ballot in the November election, just as she has for years, with her vote counting on a long list of North Carolina contests that were settled soon after.
item.dc:creator: BY GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: The resistance efforts in California, New York and other states are a counterpoint to the many Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump surveyed disaster zones in California and North Carolina on Friday and said he was considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency, offering the latest sign of how he is weighing sweeping changes to the nation's central organization for
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation's defense secretary late Friday in a dramatic tie-breaking vote, swatting back questions about his qualifications to lead the Pentagon amid allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Trump leans in on targeting Russian oil revenue as he tries to fulfill pledge to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is emphasizing that targeting Russia's oil revenue is the best way to get Moscow to end its nearly three-year war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Bishop Mariann Budde, former Minnesota bishop, tells NPR ‘I won’t apologize’ for sermon addressing Trump
item.description: The Episcopal bishop of Washington spoke with NPR to discuss her sermon addressing President Trump and why she asked him to have mercy.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
